creator: alane@geeksrus.net
text:
On Wed, Dec 05, 2001 at 12:36:34PM -0500, Vivek Khera wrote:
>
>>Number:         32538
>>Category:       ports
>>Synopsis:       kdegraphics2 has extra program in pkg-plist
>>Confidential:   no
>>Severity:       non-critical
>>Priority:       medium
>>Responsible:    freebsd-ports
>>State:          open
>>Quarter:        
>>Keywords:       
>>Date-Required:
>>Class:          sw-bug
>>Submitter-Id:   current-users
>>Arrival-Date:   Wed Dec 05 09:40:01 PST 2001
>>Closed-Date:
>>Last-Modified:
>>Originator:     Vivek Khera
>>Release:        FreeBSD 4.4-STABLE i386
>>Organization:
>>Environment:
>System: FreeBSD onceler.kciLink.com 4.4-STABLE FreeBSD 4.4-STABLE #3: Wed Oct 17 12:01:32 EDT 2001 khera@yertle.kciLink.com:/u/yertle2/usr.obj/amd/onceler/u/onceler1/usr/src/sys/ONCELER i386
>	
>>Description:
>	
>pkg-plist of kdegraphics2 has bin/kooka which is not installed.
>
>>How-To-Repeat:
>	
>make package inside kdegraphics2
>
>>Fix:

Not a bug, unless it is trying to package kooka when it wasn't built.

Kooka is built if the sane-backends package is installed. If sane-backends
isn't installed, then the lines referencing kooka in plist are commented
out, according to Makefile.

I'll test this tomorrow. 

-- 
Alan Eldridge
#include <cstdlib>
free(sklyarov);
